data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: Identification and characterization of the elusive protein backbone of the immuno-dominant and specific Em2(G11) metacestode antigen of Echinococcus multilocularis
project.projectDescription: Alveolar echinococcosis (AE) caused by Echinococcus multilocularis is a severe zoonotic disease in humans. One of the major metacestode antigens of E. multilocularis is the Em2 or Em2(G11) native purified antigen. The main objective of the current study was to identify and characterize the elusive protein backbone of the specific and immuno-dominant Em2(G11) antigen of E. multilocularis. Two biological replicates were used. Following samples are submitted: European isolate (J2012) - Replicate 1 (Sample F284313): 20200127_08_J2012_1F.raw European isolate (J2012) - Replicate 2 (Sample F284314): 20200127_09_J2012_2F.raw Central Asian isolate (AT17) - Replicate 1 (Sample F284307): 20200127_10_AT17_1F.raw Central Asian isolate (AT17) - Replicate 2 (Sample F284308): 20200127_11_AT17_2F.raw
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The mucin-type Em2 antigen samples (2 biological and 2 technical replicates) were deglycosylated and tryptic digested by the FASP protocol (Wiśniewski, 2018. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-1-4939-8695-8_1), following desalting using ZioTip C18 pipette tips. Data dependent analysis (DDA) was run on an Orbitrap Fusion LUMOS Tribrid mass spectrometer (ThermoFisher Scientific, USA) operated in conjunction with nanoAcquity UPLC M-class system and a symmetry C18 trap column and HSS T3 analytical column (Waters, USA).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Available Echinococcus spp. reference proteomes were obtained from the UniProt and WormBase Parasites databases. Proteomes of E. multilocularis (UP000017246), and the closely related taxa of E. granulosus sensu stricto G1 (UP000492820) and E. canadensis s.l. G7 (PRJEB8992) were included for the protein identification, completed with an in-house database for common contaminants. Data was analysed in mascot with protein threshold set to 95%, minimum peptide identifications to two, and peptide threshold set to 1.0% false discovery rate (FDR). Best hits have high proline and threonine concentrations, as indicated by  Hülsmeier et al., 2002 (https://doi.org/10.1074/jbc.M107161200). Relative protein abundance by label-free quantification (LFQ) of the initial affinity-purified Em2(G11) proteins was performed in MaxQuant (version 2.3.1.0).
